title: Robust Earthy Ethiopian Lentils Shiro
description: A hearty and earthy Ethiopian dish featuring lentils, bottarga, and spices, cooked in a traditional tajine clay vessel. This low-carb friendly meal is perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tajine clay vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional minute.
Add the berbere spice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the rinsed lentils, bottarga, and fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the vessel and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Serve the shiro hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Let the shiro cool, then refrigerate or freeze for meal prep.
